nodes.label: Nvidia
nodes.description: Designs the GPUs that power AI training and inference. Every major AI system runs on NVDA silicon.
nodes.chain_note: The origin node of the AI compute chain.

nodes.label: AMD
nodes.description: CPUs and GPUs competing directly with NVDA in AI training. MI300X is their flagship AI chip.
nodes.chain_note: Primary competition to NVDA. When NVDA supply tightens, AMD benefits.

nodes.label: Intel
nodes.description: CPUs and Gaudi AI accelerators. Struggling to compete in AI GPUs but dominant in server CPUs.
nodes.chain_note: Lagging NVDA in AI silicon. Turnaround story.

nodes.label: Microsoft
nodes.description: Azure cloud and OpenAI partnership. Largest enterprise buyer of NVDA compute.
nodes.chain_note: When MSFT AI revenue grows, it confirms the AI spending cycle is intact.

nodes.label: Amazon
nodes.description: AWS cloud, Bedrock AI platform, and Trainium custom AI chips.
nodes.chain_note: Second largest hyperscaler AI buyer. Also building custom silicon to reduce NVDA dependency.

nodes.label: Alphabet
nodes.description: Google Cloud, Gemini AI, and TPU custom chips. Both buyer and competitor in AI infrastructure.
nodes.chain_note: Builds its own TPUs to reduce NVDA dependency. Google Cloud is key revenue driver.

nodes.label: Meta
nodes.description: Llama open-source AI, MTIA custom chips. One of the largest GPU cluster operators globally.
nodes.chain_note: Massive capex buyer. When META guides capex higher, the whole compute chain benefits.

nodes.label: Oracle
nodes.description: OCI cloud growing fast as second-tier alternative to AWS/Azure. GPU clusters for enterprise AI.
nodes.chain_note: Beneficiary of enterprises that want AI infrastructure outside the hyperscaler trio.

nodes.label: Salesforce
nodes.description: Agentforce AI platform embedded into enterprise CRM. AI monetisation story.
nodes.chain_note: Enterprise AI software. Downstream of the AI infrastructure buildout.

nodes.label: ServiceNow
nodes.description: AI-native workflow automation for enterprises. One of the strongest AI software charts.
nodes.chain_note: Enterprise AI software beneficiary. Workflow automation expanding into AI agents.

nodes.label: Palantir
nodes.description: AI platform for government and enterprise. AIP product driving commercial growth.
nodes.chain_note: Defence and enterprise AI. Government contracts anchor the business.

nodes.label: TSMC
nodes.description: Manufactures NVDA, AMD, and Apple chips. The world's most advanced semiconductor foundry.
nodes.chain_note: Every NVDA GPU starts here. TSMC order volume is a leading indicator of AI chip demand.

nodes.label: ASML
nodes.description: Makes the EUV lithography machines that TSMC uses. Monopoly position in advanced chip manufacturing equipment.
nodes.chain_note: One step upstream of TSMC. TSMC capacity expansion = ASML machine orders.

nodes.label: Applied Materials
nodes.description: Semiconductor equipment — deposition, etching, inspection. Essential for every chip fab.
nodes.chain_note: Benefits from any fab capacity expansion, including TSMC and Intel.

nodes.label: KLA Corp
nodes.description: Process control and inspection equipment for fabs. Every defect caught before shipping.
nodes.chain_note: Picks-and-shovels for chip manufacturing. Revenue tied to fab utilisation.

nodes.label: Lam Research
nodes.description: Etch and deposition equipment. Works alongside AMAT in every leading-edge fab.
nodes.chain_note: Foundry equipment duopoly with AMAT. AI capex benefits both.

nodes.label: Cadence
nodes.description: EDA software — designs the chip before it goes to fab. Every custom AI chip uses Cadence or Synopsys.
nodes.chain_note: Design tools are the first step in any new chip. AI chip proliferation = Cadence revenue.

nodes.label: Synopsys
nodes.description: EDA and chip simulation software. Duopoly with Cadence in the design tool market.
nodes.chain_note: Every AI chip designed using Synopsys or Cadence tools.

nodes.label: Micron
nodes.description: HBM memory for AI GPUs and DRAM for data centres. HBM3E powers NVDA H200.
nodes.chain_note: AI GPU demand is HBM demand. Micron is the US memory supplier.

nodes.label: Broadcom
nodes.description: Networking chips and custom AI silicon (XPUs for Google and Meta). Dual role: enabler and competitor.
nodes.chain_note: Networking backbone of every GPU cluster. Also building custom AI chips for hyperscalers.

nodes.label: Marvell
nodes.description: Custom AI silicon and data centre networking. Growing fast as hyperscalers diversify from NVDA.
nodes.chain_note: Custom silicon for AWS and other hyperscalers. Direct NVDA alternative path.

nodes.label: Arista Networks
nodes.description: High-speed networking switches for AI clusters. 400G and 800G ethernet connecting thousands of GPUs.
nodes.chain_note: Every large GPU cluster needs Arista switching. AI scale = networking revenue.

nodes.label: Dell
nodes.description: Integrates NVDA GPUs into AI servers sold to enterprises. PowerEdge servers dominate the market.
nodes.chain_note: AI server demand amplifier. DELL earnings confirm enterprise AI spend is real.

nodes.label: HPE
nodes.description: Enterprise servers and supercomputers. Cray supercomputers for AI research.
nodes.chain_note: Enterprise AI infrastructure. Competitor to DELL in AI server market.

nodes.label: Super Micro
nodes.description: Fastest ship-times for GPU servers. Preferred integrator for AI startups and hyperscalers.
nodes.chain_note: SMCI moves with NVDA earnings and DELL sympathy. First mover in GPU server delivery.

nodes.label: Nebius
nodes.description: GPU cloud for AI developers. NVDA-backed. Growing rapidly in European AI infrastructure.
nodes.chain_note: NVDA investment. When AI cloud demand grows, NBIS is a leveraged play on it.

nodes.label: Vertiv
nodes.description: Power management and liquid cooling for data centres. Every AI GPU rack needs Vertiv equipment.
nodes.chain_note: AI data centre build-out = Vertiv revenue. Direct capex beneficiary.

nodes.label: Eaton
nodes.description: Electrical equipment and power management. Data centre power distribution.
nodes.chain_note: Infrastructure layer of AI capex. Benefits from data centre construction wave.

nodes.label: Constellation Energy
nodes.description: Nuclear power operator. Signed PPAs with hyperscalers including restarting Three Mile Island for Microsoft.
nodes.chain_note: Hyperscalers need 24/7 clean power. Nuclear is the only 24/7 clean source. CEG is the largest US nuclear operator.

nodes.label: Vistra
nodes.description: Power generation including nuclear. Signed data centre power agreements.
nodes.chain_note: Competitor to CEG for AI data centre power contracts.

nodes.label: NRG Energy
nodes.description: Power generation. Growing data centre power contracts.
nodes.chain_note: AI power generation beneficiary.

nodes.label: Equinix
nodes.description: The largest data centre REIT. Co-location facilities where AI compute lives.
nodes.chain_note: AI infrastructure real estate. Every hyperscaler cluster needs physical space.

nodes.label: Digital Realty
nodes.description: Data centre REIT. Builds and leases AI-ready facilities to hyperscalers.
nodes.chain_note: Data centre real estate beneficiary. Slower moving than pure tech but real capex chain.

nodes.label: Quanta Services
nodes.description: Builds electrical infrastructure and data centres. Electricians and engineers for the AI build-out.
nodes.chain_note: Construction pick-and-shovel for AI capex. Revenue tied to data centre build timelines.

nodes.label: EMCOR
nodes.description: Mechanical and electrical construction. Installs power and cooling in data centres.
nodes.chain_note: EMCOR builds the infrastructure inside the data centres Equinix and DLR own.

nodes.label: CrowdStrike
nodes.description: AI-native cybersecurity platform. Falcon platform uses AI for threat detection.
nodes.chain_note: Every AI-connected enterprise needs security. CRWD is the AI era security leader.

nodes.label: Palo Alto
nodes.description: Cybersecurity platform consolidation play. Competing with CRWD for AI-era security budgets.
nodes.chain_note: Enterprise security spending companion to AI adoption.

nodes.label: Ciena
nodes.description: Optical networking equipment. As AI clusters scale, fibre optic bandwidth becomes critical.
nodes.chain_note: Long-haul and metro optical networking. AI data centre interconnect demand.

nodes.label: Coherent
nodes.description: Optical components and transceivers. Copper hits physics limits above ~10k GPUs — photonics is next.
nodes.chain_note: Non-obvious AI beneficiary. As GPU clusters scale past copper limits, optical interconnects become mandatory.

nodes.label: Lumentum
nodes.description: Laser and optical components. Supplies optical transceivers for data centre interconnects.
nodes.chain_note: Same photonics chain as COHR. Signal travels last but the tailwind is structural.

nodes.label: Iris Energy
nodes.description: Bitcoin mining company pivoting to AI GPU cloud. NVDA-backed infrastructure.
nodes.chain_note: NVDA investment. GPU cloud for AI inference. Leveraged to AI compute demand.

nodes.label: Rocket Lab
nodes.description: Small satellite launch vehicles. Electron rocket and forthcoming Neutron.
nodes.chain_note: Most active small-launch operator. Revenue tied to satellite deployment demand.

nodes.label: Planet Labs
nodes.description: Daily earth observation imagery from 200+ satellites. Agriculture, defence, intelligence.
nodes.chain_note: Revenue from satellite imagery subscriptions. Defence and agriculture customers.

nodes.label: Spire Global
nodes.description: Weather and maritime tracking from space. Data-as-a-service satellite company.
nodes.chain_note: Satellite data company. Maritime AIS and weather data streams.

nodes.label: Momentus
nodes.description: In-space transportation — last-mile delivery for satellites.
nodes.chain_note: Fills the gap between launch and final orbit. Growing with small satellite demand.

nodes.label: Lockheed
nodes.description: F-35, missile systems, and space vehicles. One of two dominant US defence primes.
nodes.chain_note: Defence budget beneficiary. Space systems division growing with government contracts.

nodes.label: Northrop
nodes.description: B-21 bomber, space satellites, missile defence. The other dominant defence prime.
nodes.chain_note: Space systems and hypersonics. Stealth and satellite technology.

nodes.label: RTX
nodes.description: Raytheon missiles, Pratt engines. Missiles and propulsion systems.
nodes.chain_note: Missile systems and jet engines. Ukraine conflict expanded the order book.

nodes.label: L3Harris
nodes.description: Communications and electronic warfare for military. Space sensor systems.
nodes.chain_note: Electronic warfare and military communications. Space intelligence systems.

nodes.label: Viasat
nodes.description: Satellite internet and communications. Military satellite networks.
nodes.chain_note: Satellite connectivity. Government and commercial customers.

nodes.label: Iridium
nodes.description: Satellite phone network. True global coverage including polar regions.
nodes.chain_note: Defence and maritime communications. Monopoly on global satellite voice.

nodes.label: Moog
nodes.description: Precision flight control systems for rockets, satellites, and defence.
nodes.chain_note: Components supplier to both launch vehicles and missiles. Both space and defence exposure.

nodes.label: Curtiss-Wright
nodes.description: Defence electronics and industrial controls. Ruggedised systems for harsh environments.
nodes.chain_note: Defence electronics. Naval and aerospace systems.

nodes.label: Heico
nodes.description: FAA-approved aircraft parts at lower cost. Aerospace component aftermarket.
nodes.chain_note: Aerospace aftermarket. Benefits from both commercial and military aircraft growth.

nodes.label: Teledyne
nodes.description: High-performance sensors, cameras, and electronics for space and defence.
nodes.chain_note: Space-grade sensors. Cameras on many NASA missions and military satellites.

nodes.label: Hexcel
nodes.description: Carbon fibre composites for aerospace. Lighter rockets need advanced composites.
nodes.chain_note: Materials pick-and-shovel. Every rocket body and satellite structure uses composites.

nodes.label: ATI Inc
nodes.description: Titanium and specialty alloys for aerospace and defence.
nodes.chain_note: Materials layer. Titanium for rocket engines, airframes, and hypersonic systems.

nodes.label: Kratos
nodes.description: Drone warfare systems, satellite ground stations, and jet-powered drones.
nodes.chain_note: Low-cost drone attrition warfare. Ground stations for satellite command.

nodes.label: Axon
nodes.description: Tasers, body cameras, and drone-based public safety systems.
nodes.chain_note: Defence-tech crossover. Drone and AI for public safety.

nodes.label: Freeport-McMoRan
nodes.description: World's largest publicly traded copper producer. Grasberg mine in Indonesia is one of the world's largest copper/gold deposits. AI data centre power cables, transformers, and cooling systems all require massive copper volumes.
nodes.chain_note: Copper is the physical backbone of the AI power build-out. FCX is the primary swing producer.

nodes.label: Teck Resources
nodes.description: Canadian miner producing copper, zinc, and steelmaking coal. QB2 copper mine in Chile is its flagship growth asset.
nodes.chain_note: Major copper producer with leverage to EV and infrastructure demand.

nodes.label: MP Materials
nodes.description: Operates the only active rare earth mine in the US (Mountain Pass, California). Produces neodymium-praseodymium (NdPr) for permanent magnets used in EV motors and wind turbines.
nodes.chain_note: US rare earth sovereignty play. NdPr magnets are in every EV drivetrain.

nodes.label: Albemarle
nodes.description: Largest lithium producer by market cap. Supplies lithium for EV batteries and energy storage. Customers include most major battery makers.
nodes.chain_note: Lithium demand is a direct proxy for EV adoption rates.

nodes.label: SQM
nodes.description: Chilean lithium producer operating in the Atacama salt flat. Second largest lithium producer globally.
nodes.chain_note: Atacama brine is the lowest-cost lithium source globally.

nodes.label: Vale
nodes.description: Brazilian mining giant. World's largest iron ore producer and major nickel producer. Nickel is critical for EV battery cathodes.
nodes.chain_note: Nickel supply is a key constraint on EV battery energy density improvements.

nodes.label: Rio Tinto
nodes.description: Anglo-Australian miner. Major copper, aluminium, iron ore, and lithium producer. Jadar lithium project in Serbia is a major upcoming supply source.
nodes.chain_note: Diversified miner with exposure across the critical minerals supply chain.

nodes.label: Alcoa
nodes.description: World's largest aluminium producer. Supplies aluminium for aerospace, automotive, and data centre enclosures.
nodes.chain_note: Aluminium is the structural metal of the energy transition and defence buildout.

nodes.label: Century Aluminum
nodes.description: US primary aluminium smelter. Benefits from reshoring demand and defence aluminium requirements.
nodes.chain_note: Domestic aluminium supply for reshoring and defence industrial base.

nodes.label: Energy Fuels
nodes.description: US uranium and rare earth producer. Produces uranium for nuclear power. Also produces rare earth oxides at White Mesa Mill.
nodes.chain_note: Dual exposure: uranium for the nuclear power renaissance + rare earth for EV/defence magnets.

nodes.label: First Solar
nodes.description: US manufacturer of thin-film CdTe solar panels. Largest US solar panel maker, benefiting from IRA domestic content incentives.
nodes.chain_note: Beneficiary of IRA domestic manufacturing credits. Avoids Chinese polysilicon supply chain.

nodes.label: Enphase Energy
nodes.description: Designs microinverters for residential solar. Each panel gets its own inverter. Market leader in US residential solar.
nodes.chain_note: Residential solar platform. Microinverter architecture provides higher yield and monitoring vs string inverters.

nodes.label: Brookfield Renewable
nodes.description: Owner-operator of renewable energy assets (hydro, wind, solar). Large buyer of solar panels and wind turbines.
nodes.chain_note: Pure-play renewable energy operator. Power purchase agreements with data centres and utilities.

nodes.label: FREYR Battery
nodes.description: Norwegian battery manufacturer building gigafactories in the US and Norway. Targets utility-scale storage and EV batteries.
nodes.chain_note: Utility-scale battery storage is the grid-stabilisation layer for intermittent renewables.

nodes.label: QuantumScape
nodes.description: Solid-state lithium-metal battery developer backed by Volkswagen. Pre-revenue, developing batteries with higher energy density and safety than current Li-ion.
nodes.chain_note: If solid-state succeeds, it obsoletes current Li-ion chemistry and reshapes the entire battery supply chain.

nodes.label: Tesla
nodes.description: Designs and manufactures EVs, battery storage (Megapack), and solar products. Operates Gigafactories globally for vertical integration.
nodes.chain_note: Largest EV demand signal globally. TSLA delivery numbers move the entire EV supply chain.

nodes.label: Rivian
nodes.description: EV maker focused on trucks and commercial delivery vans. Amazon is a major investor and customer for delivery vans.
nodes.chain_note: Commercial EV fleet play. Amazon's 100k van order is the anchor contract.

nodes.label: ChargePoint
nodes.description: Operates the largest EV charging network in North America and Europe. B2B model selling hardware and software subscriptions to businesses and fleets.
nodes.chain_note: EV charging infrastructure is the utility layer enabling mass EV adoption.

nodes.label: Blink Charging
nodes.description: Owns and operates EV charging stations. Smaller than ChargePoint, more hardware-focused.
nodes.chain_note: Level 2 and DC fast charging operator. Smaller network than ChargePoint or EVgo.

nodes.label: EVgo
nodes.description: Operates DC fast charging network. Focus on highway corridors and urban locations.
nodes.chain_note: DC fast charging at scale. GM partnership anchors fleet charging contracts.

nodes.label: NextEra Energy
nodes.description: Largest electric utility in the US. World's largest generator of renewable energy (wind and solar). Also operates Florida Power & Light.
nodes.chain_note: NextEra's capex is the largest single buyer signal in the renewable energy supply chain.

nodes.label: Duke Energy
nodes.description: Large US electric utility serving the Southeast and Midwest. Transitioning from coal to natural gas and renewables.
nodes.chain_note: Traditional utility transitioning to clean energy. Rate base growth underpins steady earnings.

nodes.label: Southern Company
nodes.description: US utility operating nuclear, gas, and solar assets. Vogtle units 3 and 4 are the first new US nuclear plants in decades.
nodes.chain_note: Vogtle nuclear expansion is the proof-of-concept for new US nuclear construction.

nodes.label: Exact Sciences
nodes.description: Makes Cologuard, the non-invasive colorectal cancer screening test. Also Oncotype DX for cancer genomics.
nodes.chain_note: Precision diagnostics enabling earlier disease detection without invasive procedures.

nodes.label: Illumina
nodes.description: Dominates the DNA sequencing market. Its sequencers are used in drug discovery, oncology diagnostics, and precision medicine. GRAIL cancer detection spinout re-acquired.
nodes.chain_note: The picks-and-shovels of genomics. Every genome sequenced runs on Illumina hardware.

nodes.label: Recursion Pharma
nodes.description: Combines biology and AI to accelerate drug discovery. Partners with NVIDIA to use GPU compute for phenomics at scale.
nodes.chain_note: AI meets pharma R&D. NVDA partnership makes this a node bridging AI and healthcare clusters.

nodes.label: Seer Bio
nodes.description: Proteomics platform for protein profiling at scale. Focused on protein biomarker discovery.
nodes.chain_note: Proteomics complements genomics — proteins are the functional molecules genomics predicts.

nodes.label: Novo Nordisk
nodes.description: Danish pharma giant. Makes Ozempic and Wegovy (semaglutide, GLP-1 agonists). Largest European company by market cap. Insulin market leader for over a century.
nodes.chain_note: GLP-1 anchor. Ozempic demand is reshaping the entire healthcare supply chain from CROs to distributors.

nodes.label: Eli Lilly
nodes.description: Makes Mounjaro and Zepbound (tirzepatide, GLP-1/GIP dual agonist). US pharma giant with the most valuable GLP-1 pipeline.
nodes.chain_note: Tirzepatide shows superior weight loss vs semaglutide in head-to-head trials.

nodes.label: Viking Therapeutics
nodes.description: Clinical-stage biotech with VK2735 oral GLP-1 candidate. Potentially easier to manufacture and administer than injectable semaglutide.
nodes.chain_note: Oral GLP-1 could massively expand the addressable market by removing injection barrier.

nodes.label: Amgen
nodes.description: Large-cap biotech with MariTide (GLP-1/GIPR) in pipeline. Also major biologic drugs including Enbrel and Prolia.
nodes.chain_note: Late GLP-1 entrant with strong manufacturing and distribution capabilities.

nodes.label: IQVIA
nodes.description: World's largest CRO and healthcare data company. Runs clinical trials for NVO, LLY, AMGN, VKTX. Also has a massive real-world health data business.
nodes.chain_note: Every GLP-1 trial runs through a CRO. IQVIA is the largest and most diversified.

nodes.label: Medpace
nodes.description: Mid-size CRO specialising in metabolic, cardiovascular, and oncology trials. Known for high quality and biotech focus.
nodes.chain_note: Specialty CRO. Metabolic disease focus puts it directly in the GLP-1 trial wave.

nodes.label: ICON PLC
nodes.description: Global CRO headquartered in Ireland. One of the top 3 CROs globally alongside IQVIA and Labcorp.
nodes.chain_note: Scale CRO with global trial execution capability.

nodes.label: Charles River Labs
nodes.description: Preclinical CRO providing animal models, safety testing, and early-stage research services to pharma companies.
nodes.chain_note: Every drug starts at preclinical. Charles River is the upstream CRO before IQV and MEDP.

nodes.label: Dexcom
nodes.description: Makes continuous glucose monitors (CGMs). Ozempic/Wegovy patients often pair CGM with GLP-1 therapy for metabolic management. Core product is the G7 sensor.
nodes.chain_note: CGM adoption grows structurally with GLP-1 use. Metabolic patients need ongoing glucose visibility.

nodes.label: Abbott Labs
nodes.description: Diversified medical device and diagnostics company. Makes FreeStyle Libre CGM competing with Dexcom. Also diagnostics, cardiac devices, and nutrition.
nodes.chain_note: Abbott's FreeStyle Libre competes directly with Dexcom G7 for GLP-1 patient monitoring.

nodes.label: Phreesia
nodes.description: Patient intake and engagement software for medical practices. Helps obesity clinics and primary care practices manage GLP-1 patient workflows.
nodes.chain_note: Workflow software for high-volume GLP-1 prescribing practices.

nodes.label: Insulet
nodes.description: Makes the Omnipod tubeless insulin pump. Benefits from increased diabetes management focus alongside GLP-1 therapy.
nodes.chain_note: Insulin pump paired with CGM and GLP-1 therapy for type 1 and type 2 diabetes management.

nodes.label: McKesson
nodes.description: Largest US drug distributor. Distributes approximately 30% of all pharmaceuticals in the US including Ozempic and Wegovy.
nodes.chain_note: Every Ozempic prescription flows through McKesson, Cardinal, or ABC. Distribution margin expands with GLP-1 volume.

nodes.label: Cardinal Health
nodes.description: Second largest US drug distributor. Also makes medical products.
nodes.chain_note: GLP-1 distribution volume is the single largest new revenue driver for drug distributors.

nodes.label: Cencora
nodes.description: Third major US drug distributor (formerly AmerisourceBergen). Strong in specialty and oncology drugs.
nodes.chain_note: Specialty drug distribution expertise applies directly to GLP-1 injectables.

nodes.label: CVS Health
nodes.description: Vertically integrated: pharmacy chain, PBM (Caremark), and health insurer (Aetna). Major retail outlet for GLP-1 prescriptions.
nodes.chain_note: Triple exposure: retail pharmacy fills scripts, Caremark PBM manages formulary, Aetna covers the patient.

nodes.label: HCA Healthcare
nodes.description: Largest for-profit hospital operator in the US with 180+ hospitals. Treats obesity and metabolic disease patients who may need GLP-1 therapy.
nodes.chain_note: Hospital utilisation shifts as GLP-1s reduce obesity-related comorbidity admissions.

nodes.label: Tenet Healthcare
nodes.description: Second largest for-profit hospital operator. Similar business model to HCA but smaller scale.
nodes.chain_note: For-profit hospital exposure to metabolic disease patient volumes.

nodes.label: UnitedHealth
nodes.description: Largest US health insurer by revenue. Optum health services division. Coverage decisions directly impact GLP-1 prescription volumes.
nodes.chain_note: UNH formulary decisions determine whether 50m+ Americans can afford GLP-1 therapy.

nodes.label: Teladoc Health
nodes.description: Largest US telehealth platform. Rapidly growing GLP-1 prescribing through online visits without requiring in-person appointments.
nodes.chain_note: Telehealth removes the access barrier for GLP-1 prescriptions. Volume growing rapidly.

nodes.label: Marathon Digital
nodes.description: Largest publicly traded Bitcoin miner by hashrate in North America. Operates at utility scale.
nodes.chain_note: BTC price is the primary revenue driver. Hashrate growth requires power and silicon procurement.

nodes.label: Riot Platforms
nodes.description: Major Bitcoin miner operating in Texas. Whinstone facility is one of the largest BTC mining sites in North America.
nodes.chain_note: Texas grid exposure makes RIOT sensitive to power pricing volatility.

nodes.label: CleanSpark
nodes.description: Bitcoin miner focused on sustainable energy. Operations in Georgia and other states.
nodes.chain_note: Sustainable energy focus differentiates CLSK as institutional ESG criteria tighten for BTC exposure.

nodes.label: Hut 8
nodes.description: Canadian Bitcoin miner with US operations. Also pivoting to AI compute hosting.
nodes.chain_note: BTC miner pivoting to AI compute hosting — dual revenue stream thesis as GPU demand exceeds supply.

nodes.label: Coinbase
nodes.description: Largest US crypto exchange. Also provides custody for institutional clients and the IBIT Bitcoin ETF for BlackRock.
nodes.chain_note: Coinbase is the on-ramp for institutional and retail BTC flows. Custodian of the largest BTC ETF.

nodes.label: Robinhood
nodes.description: Retail investing app with crypto trading. More retail-focused than Coinbase. Also offers stock trading.
nodes.chain_note: Retail crypto sentiment proxy. Robinhood volumes spike with BTC/ETH volatility.

nodes.label: Strategy (MicroStrategy)
nodes.description: Enterprise software company turned Bitcoin treasury vehicle. Holds the largest BTC reserve of any public company with 210k+ BTC.
nodes.chain_note: MSTR trades as a leveraged BTC proxy. Premium/discount to NAV reflects market sentiment on BTC.

nodes.label: iShares Bitcoin Trust
nodes.description: Largest Bitcoin ETF by AUM. Custodied by Coinbase. Provides institutional BTC exposure through traditional ETF channels.
nodes.chain_note: IBIT inflows are the cleanest signal of institutional BTC demand.

nodes.label: PayPal
nodes.description: Payments giant offering crypto buying and selling on its platform. Also launched PYUSD stablecoin. Bridge between traditional payments and crypto rails.
nodes.chain_note: PYUSD stablecoin positions PayPal as a bridge between TradFi and crypto settlement rails.

nodes.label: Mastercard
nodes.description: Global payments network. Partnering with crypto companies to issue crypto-backed cards. Infrastructure for stablecoin settlement pilots.
nodes.chain_note: Mastercard is building stablecoin settlement rails while defending its core card network.

nodes.label: NXP Semiconductors
nodes.description: Makes secure element chips for contactless payments (NFC), smart cards, and crypto hardware wallets. Every payment terminal uses NXP silicon.
nodes.chain_note: NXP silicon is inside every contactless payment device globally.

nodes.label: Nucor
nodes.description: Largest US steel producer by capacity. Uses electric arc furnaces which are more flexible and lower carbon than blast furnaces. Supplies steel for construction, automotive, and infrastructure.
nodes.chain_note: EAF steel is the domestic reshoring metal. Nucor's flexibility makes it the primary beneficiary of US manufacturing growth.

nodes.label: Steel Dynamics
nodes.description: Second largest US EAF steel producer. Flat roll, structural, and rail steel products.
nodes.chain_note: EAF steel with flat-rolled focus for automotive and construction. Highly cash-generative business.

nodes.label: Reliance Steel
nodes.description: Largest US metals service center. Buys from mills, processes and distributes steel and aluminium to manufacturers.
nodes.chain_note: Metals distribution is the value-added step between mill output and factory input.

nodes.label: Vulcan Materials
nodes.description: Largest US producer of construction aggregates (crushed stone, sand, gravel). Infrastructure and construction projects require massive aggregate volumes.
nodes.chain_note: Aggregates are the first physical input in every infrastructure project. VMC pricing power is structural.

nodes.label: Martin Marietta
nodes.description: Second largest US aggregate producer. Also produces cement and ready-mix concrete.
nodes.chain_note: Aggregates and cement duopoly with VMC. Infrastructure bill spending directly increases volumes.

nodes.label: AECOM
nodes.description: Global infrastructure engineering and design firm. Designs bridges, highways, water systems, and industrial facilities.
nodes.chain_note: Engineering design is the first phase of every infrastructure project. AECOM backlog is a 2-3 year revenue visibility indicator.

nodes.label: ICF International
nodes.description: Management and technology consulting firm. Works on government infrastructure, energy transition, and reshoring policy programs.
nodes.chain_note: Government consulting on infrastructure and climate policy. Beneficiary of federal programme spending.

nodes.label: KBR
nodes.description: Engineering and construction for government, defence, and energy sectors. Specialises in ammonia/hydrogen plants and military logistics.
nodes.chain_note: Hydrogen and ammonia EPC plus defence logistics. Dual exposure to energy transition and defence spending.

nodes.label: Primoris Services
nodes.description: Specialty contractor for energy, utility, and industrial construction. Builds pipelines, solar, and data centre power infrastructure.
nodes.chain_note: Specialty construction directly in the path of data centre power and renewable energy build-out.

nodes.label: Fluor
nodes.description: Global EPC contractor. Builds petrochemical plants, semiconductor fabs, and mining facilities.
nodes.chain_note: EPC for large complex industrial projects. Semiconductor fab construction (Intel, TSMC US) is a new revenue stream.

nodes.label: MDU Resources
nodes.description: Diversified utility and construction services company. Builds utility infrastructure and pipeline construction.
nodes.chain_note: Utility infrastructure construction. Pipeline and power infrastructure build-out exposure.

nodes.label: Rockwell Automation
nodes.description: Leader in industrial automation and digital manufacturing. Connects factory equipment with software and IT systems. Key to reshoring because automated factories reduce labour cost disadvantage.
nodes.chain_note: Factory automation is what makes US reshoring economically viable versus low-wage offshore production.

nodes.label: Honeywell
nodes.description: Diversified industrial conglomerate: building controls, aerospace, performance materials, and process automation. Makes safety and control systems for factories and refineries.
nodes.chain_note: Process automation and industrial software are the productivity multipliers in reshored factories.

nodes.label: Emerson Electric
nodes.description: Process automation and precision measurement for manufacturing, energy, and life sciences. Acquired AspenTech for industrial software.
nodes.chain_note: Process control software and hardware for chemical, energy, and life science manufacturing.

nodes.label: Parker Hannifin
nodes.description: Motion and control technologies: hydraulics, pneumatics, electromechanical. Used in aerospace, industrial machinery, and EVs.
nodes.chain_note: Motion control is the physical execution layer of factory automation.

nodes.label: Illinois Tool Works
nodes.description: Diversified manufacturer of fasteners, welding equipment, food equipment, and specialty coatings. Very high margins through 80/20 portfolio strategy.
nodes.chain_note: ITW fasteners and welding equipment are inside every piece of heavy equipment and aerospace structure.

nodes.label: Caterpillar
nodes.description: World's largest construction and mining equipment manufacturer. Diesel engines, excavators, bulldozers, mining trucks. Major beneficiary of infrastructure spending.
nodes.chain_note: CAT equipment demand is the physical expression of infrastructure and mining capex cycles.

nodes.label: Deere & Co
nodes.description: Makes John Deere tractors, combines, and construction equipment. Precision agriculture leader with autonomous equipment. Overlaps with CAT in construction equipment.
nodes.chain_note: Precision agriculture automation is the food-security dimension of the reshoring/autonomy theme.

nodes.label: UPS
nodes.description: Second largest US package delivery company. Operates air and ground networks. Beneficiary of reshoring as domestic manufacturing creates more B2B freight.
nodes.chain_note: Domestic manufacturing growth increases B2B parcel and freight volumes structurally.

nodes.label: FedEx
nodes.description: Express delivery and freight. FedEx Express (air), FedEx Ground, FedEx Freight. Key logistics partner for manufacturing supply chains.
nodes.chain_note: FedEx Freight serves manufacturers shipping industrial goods. Air express critical for JIT manufacturing.

nodes.label: XPO
nodes.description: Less-than-truckload (LTL) freight specialist. Serves manufacturers and distributors shipping pallets of industrial goods.
nodes.chain_note: LTL is the freight mode of reshored manufacturing. Palletised industrial goods move via LTL.

nodes.label: C.H. Robinson
nodes.description: Largest North American freight broker. Connects shippers (manufacturers) with carriers. Asset-light model.
nodes.chain_note: Freight brokerage scales with manufacturing activity without requiring asset investment.

nodes.label: Intuitive Machines
nodes.description: Operates lunar landers for NASA. Pioneer in cislunar logistics and lunar surface operations, positioning for future rare earth and helium-3 extraction from the Moon.
nodes.chain_note: Represents the frontier of critical mineral extraction beyond Earth.

nodes.label: Flux Power
nodes.description: Makes lithium-ion battery packs for industrial equipment — forklifts, ground support, and industrial vehicles. Replacing lead-acid batteries in warehouse and logistics fleets.
nodes.chain_note: Industrial battery adoption node — downstream of lithium mining.

nodes.label: Nikola
nodes.description: Makes hydrogen fuel cell and battery-electric Class 8 semi-trucks. Financially troubled but represents hydrogen fuel cell commercialisation attempt for heavy transport.
nodes.chain_note: Hydrogen/battery truck bet — upstream dependency on hydrogen infrastructure and battery supply chain.
